name: Vegetable and Pork Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Baby carrots (or carrots cut into 2-inch pieces)
2 cups Fresh pea pods, halved crosswise (OR 6 oz Frozen pea pods, thawed and halved crosswise)
1 can Oyster, straw, or shiitake mushrooms, drained and cut into bite-size pieces (15 oz)
2 cups Bok choy or cabbage, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Partially freeze pork; cut on the bias into thin, bite-size strips.
2. Place pork in a plastic bag set in a shallow baking dish.
3. Combine sherry, hoisin sauce, ginger, garlic, and 1/2 cup water; pour over pork.
4. Close bag and marinate meat for 15 minutes at room temperature or 1 to 2 hours in refrigerator.
5. Meanwhile, cook carrots, covered, in a small amount of boiling salted water for 5 minutes; drain.
6. Drain pork; save marinade. Stir cornstarch into marinade and set aside.
7. Spray a wok or 12-inch skillet with nonstick spray coating.
8. Preheat over high heat.
9. Add carrots; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
10. Add pea pods; stir-fry for 2 minutes more.
11. Remove vegetables from the wok.
12. Add oil to the wok.
13. Stir-fry pork, half at a time, until no pink remains, 2 to 3 minutes.
14. Return all of the pork to wok; push away from center.
15. Stir marinade mixture and add to center of wok. Cook and stir until bubbly.
16. Cook and stir for 1 minute more.
17. Return vegetables to wok; add mushrooms.
18. Cook and stir for 1 minute more.
19. Serve over bok choy or cabbage leaves.
